# Coding Standards for Generated Files

Every file generated by this skill MUST follow these rules.

## Mandatory Rules

- **No new root files/folders** — all generated files go into existing directories
- **ES6 imports** — use `import`/`export`, never `require`
- **Import order** — alphabetical A-Z by import source
- **One default export** per file (except barrel `index.js` files which use named exports)
- **Max 40-50 lines** per file — if a file exceeds this, split it
- **Use `@aliases`** for cross-directory imports: `@controllers`, `@models`, `@functions`, `@filters`, `@schemas`, `@middleware`
- **Relative paths** only within the same directory (e.g. `./safe-schema` inside `schemas/`)
- **No new npm packages** — only use existing dependencies
- **English messages** for all user-facing strings

## Controller Rules

- **No `try/catch`** — the app uses `express-async-errors` which catches thrown errors globally
- **Return `200`** HTTP status on success (not 201 or 204)
- **End with `return`** statement
- **Use `error()`** from `@functions` to throw errors: `throw error(400, 'Message')`
- **Return early** — check error conditions first, then proceed with the happy path

## Response Formats

```js
// Mutations (create, update, delete)
return res.status(200).json({
  data: document,
  message: 'Resource created successfully!',
});

// GET many (list)
// Just return the paginated result directly — paginate plugin handles the shape
return res.status(200).json(documents);

// GET one (detail)
// Return the document directly, no wrapper
return res.status(200).json(document);
```

## Naming Conventions

- **Folders/files**: kebab-case (`blog-comment`, `product-variant`)
- **CRUD keywords**: `list`, `view`, `create`, `update`, `remove`
- **Controller files**: `create.js`, `read-one.js`, `read-many.js`, `update.js`, `delete.js`
- **Route paths**: pluralized resource name (`/admin/reviews`, `/admin/blog-comments`)
- **Model name**: singular kebab-case string passed to `model()` (e.g. `'review'`, `'blog-comment'`)
- **Export names**: PascalCase for models/controllers (`Review`, `BlogComment`), camelCase for routes/filters/schemas
